title: Fresh Folded German Pork Dumplings
description: A traditional German dish featuring fresh pork and sage, these dumplings are a crowd-pleaser and can be served as a main course or side dish. The paleo-friendly ingredients make it a great option for those with dietary restrictions.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the ground pork, chopped sage, finely chopped onion, egg, salt, and black pepper, and mix well with your hands until just combined.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
When ready to cook, bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and reduce the heat to a simmer.
Using your hands, shape the pork mixture into small dumplings, about 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
Add the dumplings to the simmering water and cook for 10-12 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
While the dumplings are cooking, melt the pork fat in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Once the dumplings are cooked, remove them from the water with a slotted spoon and add them to the saucepan with the melted pork fat.
Toss the dumplings in the pork fat to coat, then sprinkle with potato starch and toss again to coat.
Serve the dumplings hot, garnished with additional chopped sage if desired.
